name: sap_hana
explanation: The SAP HANA check connects to the HANA database over the SQL protocol via the proprietary `hdbcli` driver and runs SQL queries against monitoring views (`M_*`, `SYS_DATABASES.*`). It requires a user with `MONITORING` (or equivalent) role privileges granted ahead of time. The spec marks `server`, `username`, and `password` as `required: true`, with `password` flagged `secret: true`. There is no unauthenticated query path on HANA — this is pattern D (spec-required credentials), the same shape as postgres / mysql / sqlserver.
notes: Default port 30015 is the standard SYSTEMDB SQL port (3<instance_number>15), so a network probe could trivially identify the listener — but credentials cannot be discovered. The integration also requires the operator to manually `pip install hdbcli` into the Agent's embedded Python.
